title: Laser marking focus feedback system
abstract: A method of focusing includes irradiating an object by directing radiation output by a radiating source through an objective lens, measuring a first intensity of reflected radiation that is reflected from the object, adjusting a distance between the objective lens and the object, measuring a second intensity of reflected radiation, and analyzing the first intensity of reflected radiation and the second intensity of reflected radiation to determine a focal distance between the objective lens and the object. The distance between the objective lens and the object is adjusted to the focal distance and the irradiating intensity is increased to mark the object. In another example, measuring the first intensity of reflected radiation is performed by directing reflected radiation from the object through the objective lens, a beam splitter, a focusing lens, and a pinhole and onto a sensor that outputs a signal indicative of sensed radiation intensity.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A focusing system, comprising: a radiating source; a beam splitter configured to reflect or transmit radiation output by the radiating source; an objective lens configured to be irradiated by the radiation from the beam splitter; and a sensor, wherein the sensor is configured to be irradiated by a reflected radiation that travels through the objective lens and the beam splitter, and wherein the sensor outputs a measured intensity signal that indicates an intensity of the reflected radiation. 2. The focusing system of Claim 1, wherein irradiation of an object causes the reflected radiation that irradiates the sensor. 3. The focusing system of Claim 2, wherein the object is selected from the group consisting of: a wafer, glass, plastic, quartz, sapphire, silicon, Silicon Carbide (SiC), and Gallium Nitride (GaN). 4. The focusing system of Claim 1 , further comprising: a half wave plate, wherein the half wave plate is configured such that the radiation output by the radiation source passes through the half wave plate before irradiating the beam splitter. 5. The focusing system of Claim 1, further comprising: a polarized beam splitter, wherein the polarized beam splitter is configured such that the radiation output by the radiation source passes through the polarized beam splitter before irradiating the beam splitter. 6. The focusing system of Claim 1 , further comprising: 

 a beam expander, wherein the beam expander is configured such that the radiation output by the radiation source passes through the beam expander before irradiating the objective lens. 7. The focusing system of Claim 1, further comprising: a focusing lens, wherein the focusing lens is configured such that the reflected radiation passes through the objective lens, the beam splitter, and the focusing lens before irradiating the sensor. 8. The focusing system of Claim 1, further comprising: a pinhole, wherein the pinhole is configured such that the reflected radiation passes through the objective lens, the beam splitter, and the pinhole before irradiating the sensor. 9. The focusing system of Claim 1, further comprising: a pinhole, wherein the pinhole is configured such that the reflected radiation passes through the objective lens, the beam splitter, a focusing lens, and the pinhole before irradiating the sensor. 10. The focusing system of Claim 8, wherein the pinhole is located at a position that is conjugate the position of the irradiation of the object. 11. The focusing system of Claim 1 , wherein the sensor is a photodiode, and wherein the pinhole is between five percent and two-hundred percent of the radiation beam waist diameter. 12. The focusing system of Claim 1, further comprising: an optical fiber, wherein the optical fiber is configured such that the reflected radiation passes through the objective lens, the beam splitter, and the optical fiber before irradiating the sensor. 13. An apparatus for focusing, comprising: a radiating source; a beam splitter configured to reflect radiation output by the radiating source; an objective lens configured to be irradiated by the radiation reflected by the beam splitter; a sensor, wherein the sensor is configured to be irradiated by a reflected radiation that travels through the objective lens and the beam splitter, and wherein the sensor outputs a measured intensity signal that indicates an intensity of the reflected radiation, a memory; and a processor configured to: read the measured intensity signal output by the sensor; and cause a measured intensity value to be stored in the memory. 14. The apparatus of Claim 13, wherein the processor is further configured to control a distance between the objective lens and an object to be irradiated. 15. A method of focusing, comprising: (a) irradiating an object, wherein the irradiating of (a) is performed by directing radiation output by a radiating source through an objective lens; (b) measuring a first intensity of reflected radiation that is reflected from the object; (c) adjusting a distance between the objective lens and the object; (d) measuring a second intensity of reflected radiation that is reflected from the object; and (e) analyzing the first intensity of reflected radiation and the second intensity of reflected radiation to determine a focal distance between the objective lens and the object. 16. The method of Claim IS, wherein the irradiating of (a) is performed by directing radiation output by the radiating source through a beam splitter. 17. The method of Claim 15, wherein the measuring of the first intensity of reflected radiation is performed by directing reflected radiation from the object through the 

 objective lens and a beam splitter and onto a sensor that outputs a signal indicative of sensed radiation intensity. 18. The method of Claim 15, wherein the measuring of the first intensity of reflected radiation is performed by directing reflected radiation from the object through the objective lens, a beam splitter, and a focusing lens and onto a sensor that outputs a signal indicative of sensed radiation intensity. 19. The method of Claim 15, wherein the measuring of the first intensity of reflected radiation is performed by directing reflected radiation from the object through the objective lens, a beam splitter, a focusing lens, and a pinhole and onto a sensor that outputs a signal indicative of sensed radiation intensity. 20. The method of Claim 15, wherein steps (a) through (d) are caused by a processor executing code stored in a memory, and wherein the analyzing of (e) is performed by a processor executing code stored in a memory. 21. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: (f) adjusting the distance between the objective lens and the object to the focal distance; and (g) increasing the irradiating intensity to mark the object.
